response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. EACH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R. THE SECOND CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF 1.1R, THE THIRD HAS 1.21R, AND SO ON. THIS FORMS A GEOMETRIC SEQUENCE WHERE EACH TERM IS MULTIPLIED BY 1.1. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². SO THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THIS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS πR²(1 + 1.1² + 1.1⁴ + 1.1⁶ + 1.1⁸ + 1.1¹⁰ + 1.1¹² + 1.1¹⁴). THIS SUM REPRESENTS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS CALCULATED USING THE FORMULA 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 1.1⁴R. SO THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π(1.1⁴R). EACH CHARACTER, INCLUDING SPACES, TAKES UP A TOTAL OF 0.5 CM. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE, DIVIDE THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY 0.5. THE RESULT IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE EVENLY SPACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THIS NUMBER MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE FLOOR OF THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THIS ENSURES THAT THE TOTAL SPACING IS NOT EXCEEDED. THE DESIGNER CAN THEN USE THIS NUMBER TO PLACE THE TEXT ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE FINAL ANSWER IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THIS VALUE IS AN INTEGER AND REPRESENTS THE MAXIMUM POSSIBLE NUMBER OF CHARACTERS
